sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Hologic goes private in $79 per share buyout by Blackstone, TPG
feed.summary: Hologic Inc. has transitioned to private ownership in a $79 per share buyout by funds managed by Blackstone and TPG, with minority investments from Abu Dhabi Investment Authority and GIC. Joe Almeida has been appointed CEO, replacing Stephen MacMillan. Stockholders received $76 per share in cash plus a non-tradable contingent value right of up to $3 per share, dependent on future Breast Health business revenue goals.

feed.title: Dollar Tree (DLTR) Stock Drops on Analyst Downgrades and Inflation Worries
feed.summary: Dollar Tree (DLTR) stock experienced a nearly 5% drop due to recent analyst downgrades and a cautious fiscal 2026 outlook. The decline extends a trend observed since the mid-March earnings release, which saw the company's revenue slightly miss estimates despite beating profit expectations. Macroeconomic concerns like inflation and rising oil prices are exacerbating pressure on consumer-facing stocks, contributing to the negative sentiment surrounding DLTR.

feed.title: Altria Group call volume above normal and directionally bullish
feed.summary: Altria Group (MO) is experiencing significantly higher-than-normal call option volume, with 19,443 calls trading, which is five times the expected amount. This suggests a bullish sentiment among options traders, as implied volatility for MO has increased to 27.08%. The most active options are the 4/10 weekly $70 and $68 calls, collectively representing close to 13,900 contracts, ahead of the company's earnings report on April 30th.

feed.title: Targa Resources stock hits all-time high at 253.4 USD
feed.summary: Targa Resources Inc. (TRGP) has reached an all-time high of $253.4, reflecting strong investor confidence and a 57.61% increase in stock value over the past year. The midstream energy company boasts a PEG ratio of 0.6 and a dividend yield of 1.63%, though InvestingPro analysis indicates it is currently overvalued. Analyst firms like UBS, Truist Securities, Wolfe Research, and RBC Capital have all recently raised their price targets for TRGP, citing growth prospects and strategic financial maneuvers, including a recent $1.5 billion senior notes offering.

feed.title: Mohawk Industries stock hits 52-week low at 94.56 USD
feed.summary: Mohawk Industries Inc. stock has hit a new 52-week low, trading at $95.15, following a 5.11% decline over the past year. This comes as 14 analysts have revised their earnings forecasts downwards, reflecting caution about near-term prospects despite a recent strong Q4 2025 earnings report. InvestingPro suggests the stock may be undervalued, and stakeholders are monitoring strategic moves to reverse the downward trend.

feed.title: Cisco and Quest Alliance Are Modernizing Vocational Training for India's Next-Gen Workforce
feed.summary: Cisco and Quest Alliance are partnering to modernize vocational training in India's Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs) to better prepare students for the digital economy. Their Future Right Skills Network (FRSN) initiative, co-funded by several organizations, strengthens ITIs by shaping policy, building capacity for teachers and leaders, and integrating technology-fueled career development tools. This effort directly supports over 300,000 students and has significantly increased earning potential and employability, ensuring that vocational education aligns with current industry demands and empowers students like Priya for economic mobility.

feed.title: Private credit woes are not systemic – Morgan Stanley’s Huberty
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley's Katy Huberty believes that while risks in private credit are significant, they are not systemic, anticipating a default cycle within the asset class. Despite these concerns, she expects broader financial stability remains limited. This perspective comes as private credit has experienced considerable growth and awaits a period of recalibration.

feed.title: Major Investment From Anthem Will Help VFCCE Boost Food and Basic Needs Security Across Virginia's Community Colleges
feed.summary: The Virginia Foundation for Community College Education (VFCCE) is expanding its efforts to address food and basic needs insecurity among students at Virginia’s 23 community colleges, thanks to a $100,000 investment from the Anthem Blue Cross and Blue Shield Foundation. This funding will focus on increasing student reliance on the Single Stop benefits screener, expanding local food partnerships, enhancing campus pantry access to healthy foods, and providing direct support for nutritious food to students. The partnership aims to ensure students can focus on their education, improve their health, and create stability for their families and the Commonwealth.

feed.title: Delta Amex Cards Feature as High as 100K Miles Welcome Offers
feed.summary: American Express has modified the welcome offer structure for three personal Delta credit cards, introducing variable offers that are "as high as" 100,000 SkyMiles. This means the actual bonus received will depend on individual financial profiles, with the exact offer revealed during the application process before a hard credit inquiry is made. The article details the specific cards (Delta SkyMiles Reserve, Platinum, and Gold American Express Cards) and explains how applicants can check their personalized offer without impacting their credit score.

feed.title: Minnesota-based US Bank partners with NFL
feed.summary: U.S. Bank, headquartered in Minneapolis, has announced a multi-year partnership with the NFL, becoming an official bank and wealth management sponsor. This collaboration will include sponsorship for the upcoming Super Bowl and the NFL FLAG Championships. Building on a 20-year relationship, U.S. Bank will also provide financial education expertise to help NFL players navigate their financial futures.

feed.title: Abbott Laboratories stock: Trading at a discount amid steady growth potential
feed.summary: Abbott Laboratories (ABT) stock is currently trading at a discount, approximately 23% below fair value estimates, making it an attractive option for dividend-focused investors. The company boasts a diversified healthcare portfolio, robust financial health, and a strong dividend history with 54 consecutive years of increases. Despite some recent market pressures and revenue shortfalls, analysts maintain a positive outlook due to its innovation, operational consistency, and significant long-term growth potential from global demographic shifts and personalized medicine trends.

feed.title: Murphy USA names permanent CFO
feed.summary: Murphy USA has appointed Donald R. Smith Jr. as its permanent Senior Vice President, CFO, and Treasurer, effective last Friday. Smith previously served as interim CFO and chief accounting officer since October, following Galagher Jeff's departure to Arko Corp. Smith has been with Murphy USA since its 2013 spin-off from Murphy Oil Corp.

feed.title: General Dynamics (NYSE:GD) Price Target Lowered to $380.00 at Jefferies Financial Group
feed.summary: Jefferies Financial Group has lowered its price target for General Dynamics (NYSE:GD) to $380.00 from $385.00, maintaining a "hold" rating, though this still implies an 8.97% upside. Despite the lowered target, the analyst consensus remains "Moderate Buy" with an average target of $384.16. General Dynamics recently exceeded Q4 earnings expectations with $4.17 EPS and $14.38 billion in revenue and provided strong FY2026 guidance.

feed.title: Jefferies Adjusts Price Target on First Solar to $197 From $187, Maintains Hold Rating
feed.summary: Jefferies has changed its price target for First Solar (FSLR) to $197, an increase from its previous target of $187. The firm decided to maintain its "Hold" rating on the stock. This adjustment comes after a previous revision in rating, indicating ongoing analyst evaluation of the company's performance.

feed.title: Will Strength in Application Software Drive Roper's Growth?
feed.summary: Roper Technologies (ROP) is experiencing strong growth in its Application Software segment, which contributed over half of its revenues in Q4 2025. This growth is fueled by increasing demand for SaaS solutions and GenAI innovations across its Aderant, Deltek, Vertafore, and PowerPlan businesses. The company anticipates mid-single-digit organic revenue growth for this segment in 2026, driven by steady recurring revenues.

feed.title: Paramount Skydance Stock Jumps 9% on $24B Gulf Funding for Warner Bros Takeover Bid
feed.summary: Paramount Skydance shares surged nearly 9% after reports confirmed advanced talks for $24 billion in equity commitments from Middle Eastern sovereign wealth funds to finance its $81 billion to $110 billion takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery. This funding package is expected to reduce reliance on debt and aid regulatory scrutiny, de-risking the Warner deal significantly. A special shareholder vote for Warner Bros. Discovery is scheduled for April 23 to approve the transaction, aiming to create a dominant media powerhouse.

feed.title: Intel is joining Elon Musk's $20B Terafab project that is starting in Austin
feed.summary: Intel has announced its intention to join Elon Musk's $20 billion Terafab project, which is set to begin in Austin. The project, a joint venture between Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI, aims to produce 1 terawatt of compute annually. Details about Intel's involvement were shared on social media, adding a significant name to the ambitious endeavor that Musk revealed last month at the Seaholm Power Plant.

feed.title: Marine Products Corp stock: What you need to know for smart investing now
feed.summary: Marine Products Corp (NYSE: MPX) is highlighted as a resilient small-cap manufacturer of fiberglass boats, offering Chaparral and Robalo brands. The article details its business model, financial health, competitive advantages, and the risks associated with the recreational boating industry. It suggests that MPX could be a suitable investment for those who believe in leisure economy recovery, emphasizing its lean operations, dividend payouts, and strong dealer network.

feed.title: Air Products adds liquefaction to Port of Rotterdam grey hydrogen plant
feed.summary: Air Products is installing liquefaction capacity at its grey hydrogen plant in the Port of Rotterdam, which is also being equipped with carbon capture technology. The liquid hydrogen facility, expected to be operational in 2027, will help meet demand in various sectors across Benelux, France, Germany, and the UK. The addition of liquefaction and carbon capture will enhance its ability to supply merchant hydrogen and reduce emissions.

feed.title: NFL and U.S. Bank Announce Partnership Centered on Banking and Wealth Management
feed.summary: The NFL and U.S. Bank have announced a multi-year partnership, designating U.S. Bank as an official bank and wealth management sponsor of the NFL. This collaboration builds on a 20-year relationship and includes U.S. Bank becoming the presenting sponsor of the Super Bowl MVP Award and a top-tier sponsor for the NFL FLAG Championships. The partnership will also focus on financial education for players through the U.S. Bank Financial Edge program and a co-owned Corporate Social Responsibility initiative.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Seizes Private Credit Opportunity Amid Exodus
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs' $15.7 billion private credit fund successfully mitigated the broader investor exodus experienced by its peers, thanks to its focus on institutional investors. The firm is now poised to exploit the retreat of retail money from the private credit market, with an easing of competition and improved lending terms. Other financial giants like Morgan Stanley and JPMorgan Chase are also looking to launch new funds in this space, despite warnings about potential leveraged lending losses.

feed.title: BNP Paribas Adjusts TransDigm Group Price Target to $1,700 From $1,900, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: BNP Paribas has lowered its price target for TransDigm Group (TDG) to $1,700 from $1,900 but continues to maintain an Outperform rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a revised valuation while the firm still sees strong potential for the aerospace systems and components manufacturer. The article also notes TransDigm Group's recent completion of a $2.2 billion acquisition of Jet Parts Engineering and Victor Sierra Aviation.

feed.title: Jefferies Financial Group Issues Pessimistic Forecast for First Solar (NASDAQ:FSLR) Stock Price
feed.summary: Jefferies Financial Group has reduced its price target for First Solar (NASDAQ:FSLR) from $205 to $187, maintaining a "hold" rating, which suggests a slight downside. This adjustment comes amidst mixed analyst views and after First Solar missed recent EPS estimates but exceeded revenue forecasts. Insider sales have also been noted, with approximately 75,989 shares sold over the past 90 days.

feed.title: Upstart Holdings (UPST) Could Double From Here According To Mizuho Securities
feed.summary: Mizuho Securities analyst Dan Dolev reiterated an "Outperform" rating on Upstart Holdings (UPST) and maintained a price target of $51, suggesting a potential 98.8% upside from current levels. The company recently partnered with Harborstone Credit Union to expand its personal lending services, leveraging Upstart's AI-powered platform for a more modern and digital borrowing experience. While Upstart continues to grow its AI lending platform, the article also notes that other AI stocks might offer greater upside potential with less risk.

feed.title: Why Challenger, Lotus Resources, Mesoblast, and Wildcat shares are falling today
feed.summary: Shares of Challenger, Lotus Resources, Mesoblast, and Wildcat Resources are all falling today despite a general market gain. Challenger's shares are down despite a new partnership with Bank of Queensland, while Lotus Resources is down due to a production delay at its uranium mine caused by fire damage. Mesoblast's shares are falling after a sales update for its Ryoncil treatment may have disappointed investors, and Wildcat Resources saw a decline despite a drilling update showing an extension of mineralisation at one of its projects.

feed.title: Costco hit with class action over unexpected membership renewals
feed.summary: Costco is facing a class action lawsuit alleging that it violates California law by sending untimely and deficient membership renewal notices. Plaintiff Russel George claims Costco's email notices 60 days prior to automatic renewal fall outside the state's 45-day window and lack required information about terms, charges, and cancellation methods. George, who was surprised by his January 2026 Gold Star membership renewal, is seeking a jury trial, declaratory and injunctive relief, and damages for himself and other class members.

feed.title: Moleculin Releases Next CEO Corner Segment Highlighting Annamycin’s Non-Cardiotoxic Profile
feed.summary: Moleculin Biotech, Inc. announced the release of its latest CEO Corner segment, where CEO Walter Klemp highlighted Annamycin's unique non-cardiotoxic safety profile. This differentiated characteristic, absent in traditional anthracyclines like doxorubicin, could significantly expand treatment options for patients with relapsed or refractory AML and advanced solid tumors. The company believes Annamycin's design may position it as a promising new oncology treatment, reshaping the use of anthracyclines in cancer care.

feed.title: TransDigm Group Completes $2.2 Billion Acquisition of Jet Parts Engineering and Victor Sierra Aviation Holdings
feed.summary: TransDigm Group (NYSE: TDG) has completed the acquisition of Jet Parts Engineering (JPE) and Victor Sierra Aviation Holdings (VSA) for approximately $2.2 billion in cash. This strategic move, funded by cash on hand and debt offerings, expands TransDigm's presence in the aerospace aftermarket, adding about $280 million in annual revenue to its portfolio. The acquisition strengthens TransDigm's focus on proprietary OEM-alternative parts and repair solutions for commercial, regional, and general aviation sectors.

feed.title: MDU Resources Group (NYSE:MDU) Reaches New 52-Week High - Time to Buy?
feed.summary: MDU Resources Group (NYSE:MDU) recently hit a new 52-week high of $21.50, despite a consensus "Hold" rating from Wall Street with a $21.00 price target. The company reported Q4 EPS in line with estimates and revenue slightly below, while setting FY2026 EPS guidance. Insider activity includes a director's purchase of 5,000 shares, and institutional investors hold a significant portion of the stock.

feed.title: Philips Walita Adopts Tritan Renew for Premium Blender
feed.summary: Philips Walita is enhancing its sustainability efforts by integrating Eastman Tritan Renew copolyester into the jars of its premium 5000 Series RI2242/90 blender. This switch provides certified recycled content without sacrificing performance, clarity, durability, or safety, aligning with Philips Walita's commitment to circular materials and reduced carbon emissions. Eastman's molecular recycling technology enables the use of recycled content, helping Philips Walita meet consumer demand for sustainable products and maintain product quality.

feed.title: Eversource, Avangrid ask FERC to stay $1.5B refund decision
feed.summary: Eversource and Avangrid have requested that the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) pause a decision requiring New England utilities to issue about $1.5 billion in refunds to customers for transmission charges dating back to 2011. The utilities argue that enforcing the refunds immediately could cause "rate whiplash" and irreparable harm if the decision is later overturned in court, impacting their liquidity, credit metrics, and investor confidence. While Jefferies analysts anticipate FERC will extend the refund deadline, they expect the request to stay the decision itself to be rejected.

feed.title: Synchrony tops Fortune Best Companies list
feed.summary: Synchrony, which provides financing to powersports dealers, has been ranked No. 1 on the Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For list for 2026. The company's rise to the top spot is attributed to its progressive approach to AI adoption and a culture built on trust, which has also translated into strong business performance. This recognition highlights how Synchrony's employee-first strategy and focus on innovation can benefit powersports dealerships through improved customer experience and expanded credit access.

feed.title: Deutsche Bank Upbeat On Amcor (AMCR) Despite Sector Wide Headwinds
feed.summary: Deutsche Bank has initiated coverage on Amcor plc (NYSE: AMCR) with a Buy rating and a $50 price target, despite anticipating continued sector-wide challenges for the packaging industry in 2026. The bank remains positive on rigid and flexible packaging segments while expressing caution on fiber-based packaging. This optimistic stance contrasts with Wells Fargo's recent downgrade of Amcor to Equal Weight, citing disproportionate reactions to the Iran conflict and preferring companies with strong U.S. market exposure and low leverage.

feed.title: Optimizely Recognized as a Leader for the 9th Year in a Row in Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Content Marketing Platforms
feed.summary: Optimizely has been named a Leader in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Content Marketing Platforms for the ninth consecutive year, recognizing its consistent leadership in the evolving DXP category. The company's Content Marketing Platform, powered by Optimizely Opal, leverages AI for active content planning, creation, and execution, addressing the shift from AI assistance to AI execution. This recognition highlights Optimizely's commitment to innovation and customer success in digital experience and content marketing.

feed.title: Optimizely Recognized as a Leader for the 9th Year in a Row in Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Content Marketing Platforms
feed.summary: Optimizely has been recognized as a Leader in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Content Marketing Platforms for the ninth consecutive year. This consistent recognition highlights Optimizely's ability to help enterprises plan, produce, and distribute content at scale, particularly through its AI orchestration platform, Optimizely Opal, which enables "Autonomous Ops" by embedding AI agents into CMP workflows. The company's leadership in the category reflects its shift towards AI execution in content marketing, providing speed, governance, and measurable impact for large global enterprises.

feed.title: BNP Paribas Adjusts Howmet Aerospace Price Target to $265 From $280, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: BNP Paribas has lowered its price target for Howmet Aerospace (HWM) to $265 from $280, while reaffirming its "Outperform" rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a revised valuation perspective for the aerospace and defense solutions provider. The company continues to receive attention from analysts with other recent price target adjustments and news regarding acquisitions and industry trends.

feed.title: The Dow Tests a Key Technical Threshold. Watching Apple, Home Depot Stocks.
feed.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average is testing its 200-day simple moving average, a significant technical indicator, making it the first major U.S. index to do so. Investors are closely monitoring this development to gauge if it signals a broader market uptrend or another challenge in the ongoing recovery. The article also mentions keeping an eye on Apple and Home Depot stocks.

feed.title: Nvidia’s (NVDA) Deal With Competitor Marvell (MRVL) Only Strengthens Its Own Ecosystem, Here’s How
feed.summary: NVIDIA Corporation has announced a strategic partnership with Marvell Technology, including a $2 billion investment, to integrate Marvell's custom AI accelerators (XPUs) with NVIDIA's NVLink Fusion AI infrastructure. This collaboration aims to strengthen NVIDIA's AI ecosystem by combining Marvell's XPUs and interconnect solutions with NVIDIA's extensive suite of supporting technologies. Analysts view this deal as a significant move for both companies, boosting Marvell with the investment and further solidifying NVIDIA's position in the AI market.

feed.title: Delta Air Lines (DAL) Increases Checked Bag Fees Amid Rising Fue
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines (DAL) has increased its checked baggage fees by $10, responding to rising jet fuel costs. The airline's financial health shows strong profitability and revenue growth, but also notes potential liquidity issues and a "distress zone" Altman Z-Score. Despite insider selling, analyst sentiment is cautiously optimistic, with a moderate buy recommendation.

feed.title: McEwen Copper seeks $4B to advance Los Azules
feed.summary: McEwen Copper Inc. is seeking approximately $4 billion in financing to develop its Los Azules project in Argentina, one of the country's largest undeveloped copper deposits. The company is engaging with global lenders, including export-credit agencies and financial groups in Europe and Japan, aiming for a mix of equity and equipment financing. The project, supported by a feasibility study, targets first production by 2030 and significant annual copper output, capitalizing on improving policy conditions in Argentina.

feed.title: Everest Re Group Ltd stock: What investors should know now
feed.summary: Everest Re Group Ltd is highlighted as a global reinsurance leader, offering stability and exposure to risk transfer. The article details its core business model, competitive advantages, and positive analyst views from firms like Barclays, Keefe Bruyette & Woods, and Jefferies & Co. It emphasizes Everest's relevance for investors seeking diversification, inflation hedging, and income, while also noting risks such as catastrophe losses and investment portfolio sensitivity.

feed.title: NFL and U.S. Bank Announce Partnership Centered on Banking and Wealth Management
feed.summary: The NFL and U.S. Bank have announced a multi-year partnership, naming U.S. Bank as an official bank and wealth management sponsor of the NFL. This collaboration deepens a 20-year relationship, with U.S. Bank becoming the presenting sponsor of the Super Bowl MVP Award and a top-tier sponsor of the NFL FLAG Championships. The partnership will also involve U.S. Bank providing financial education expertise to NFL players through a customized program called U.S. Bank Financial Edge™.

feed.title: Massimo announces FY25 financials, says losses reflect ‘deliberate strategy’
feed.summary: Massimo Group reported its FY2025 financial results, showing a 34.3% decrease in revenue to $71.8 million, a strategic decision to rebalance dealer inventory and prioritize pricing integrity. Despite the revenue decline, the company saw an improved gross margin of 37.5%, driven by product mix optimization and cost control. Massimo plans to expand its premium vehicle lineup, focusing on utility-driven, all-weather mobility solutions like the Sentinel Series UTVs and MVR Pro Series electric carts.

feed.title: NOV: Susquehanna Maintains Rating and Raises Price Target to $22
feed.summary: Susquehanna has maintained a positive rating on NOV and increased its price target from $21 to $22, a 4.76% rise, according to analyst Charles Minervino. This update follows several other analyst adjustments in early 2026, with most firms raising price targets while maintaining neutral or market perform ratings, and Goldman Sachs maintaining a sell rating. The average analyst price target for NOV is $20.22, suggesting a 7.39% upside from its current price.

feed.title: ON Semiconductor Corp stock: What investors should know now
feed.summary: ON Semiconductor Corp continues to navigate semiconductor headwinds, reporting an EPS beat of $0.64 against estimates of $0.62, despite an 11.2% year-over-year revenue dip. The company focuses on power management, analog, and sensor solutions, particularly for automotive and industrial sectors, leveraging silicon carbide technology. While thin margins and recent insider sales raise questions, strong liquidity, efficient capital use, and Wall Street's cautious but balanced "Hold" ratings suggest stability with potential for future upside as the company capitalizes on electrification trends.

feed.title: Kroger bringing back Senior Discount Day on April 8
feed.summary: Kroger is reintroducing its Senior Discount Day on April 8, offering a 5% discount to shoppers aged 55 and older in the Greater Cincinnati area. Shoppers need to show proof of age with a driver's license or state ID and use a Kroger Plus Card to qualify. The discount applies to all food items but excludes gift cards, alcohol, tobacco, and lottery tickets.

feed.title: Iranian Hacker Group Handala Targets Israel’s UAV Backbone with First-Ever Leaked Photo of Elbit Design Team
feed.summary: The Iranian-linked hacking group Handala has leaked an image they claim shows the core design team of the Elbit System Hermes Drone, considered central to Israel's UAV infrastructure. This act is seen by experts as an escalation in psychological warfare and targeted doxing aimed at creating fear among individuals working in Israel's defense sector. The group has previously offered bounties and issued personal threats against Israeli engineers and defense contractors.

feed.title: Leidos Holdings Inc. (LDOS) insider resale notice: 2,000 shares proposed
feed.summary: Leidos Holdings Inc. filed a Form 144 indicating a proposed sale of 2,000 shares of common stock from a vested shares account with an effective date of April 6, 2026. This regulatory notice also revealed a prior sale of 3,000 shares on January 20, 2026, for $576,635.10, attributed to Elizabeth A. Porter. The filing is a routine Section 144 notice for insider resale activity, with Citigroup Global Markets, Inc. listed as the broker for the proposed sale.

feed.title: Merck Begins Tender Offer to Acquire Terns Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
feed.summary: Merck has initiated a cash tender offer to acquire all outstanding shares of Terns Pharmaceuticals, Inc. for $53.00 per share. This follows a definitive agreement announced on March 25, 2026, and is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to customary conditions including the tender of over 50% of Terns' shares and regulatory approvals. The acquisition will make Terns a wholly owned subsidiary of Merck.

feed.title: Flying with Delta Airlines? Your luggage just got expensive — here’s how much you’ll pay
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines is increasing checked bag fees by $10 to $50 for bookings made on or after Wednesday, citing rising jet fuel costs due to Middle East tensions. This means the first checked bag will cost $45, the second $55, and the third $200. JetBlue also recently increased its baggage fees, as airlines worldwide grapple with increased operating costs.
